pages.full_text: [Photo page: a photograph of what appears to be an interior architectural scene (possibly a railway station or large hall with glass roof and metalwork), with a decorative card or item overlaid showing a dotted leaf/plant pattern in the Orla Kiely style. No written text body on this page. The far right margin shows the beginning of a numbered index list, cut off at the gutter edge — numbers 1 through approximately 27 are partially visible but the text beside each number is on the right-hand page.]
pages.notes: This page is dominated by a glued-in photograph (interior architectural scene) with a decorative dotted-leaf patterned card overlaid. The index list visible on the right edge of this image belongs to the right-hand page; it is not transcribed here to avoid duplication.

pages.notes: This is an index page — a numbered list mapping entry numbers to place names/trip destinations. Numbers 16, 17, 30, 34, 44 are absent (pages presumably not yet written or skipped). Some entries are difficult to read: entry 11 has a letter or abbreviation after 'Mannheim'; entry 12 'Ellesee' may be 'Elsee' or 'Elsässer' (Bavaria region); entry 20 second place uncertain — could be 'Iba Amena' or similar; entry 23 first word unclear — could be 'Iknage' or 'Idringe'; entry 24 'Florgue Baume' uncertain; entry 25 third place could be 'Tainc' or 'Taizé'; entry 29 second place could be 'Fochanges' or 'Frochanges'; entry 35 second place could be 'Godalford' or 'Godalming'; entry 38 'Boufon' possibly 'Buffon', 'Strogone' possibly 'Bourgogne'; entry 39 '[?Brogny]' uncertain; entry 40 'Gouaze' possibly 'Gouraze' or 'Gouarec'; entry 41 first word uncertain — '[?Bouget] Commun' possibly 'Bouquet Commun'; entry 50 '[?Hlo]' possibly an abbreviation; entry 52 '[?Watloo]' possibly 'Waterloo'; entry 54 'Haies de [?Niklotts]' and '[?Cherrheins]' very uncertain.
